person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Les armes de Courtenay se blasonnent ainsi : 
D'or à trois tourteaux de gueules, appelée besant, pièce de monnaie ronde d'or ou d'argent, frappée autrefois à Byzance (aujourd'hui Istanbul), d'où elle tire son nom. Ancienne monnaie des Empereurs de Byzance, elle évoque les croisades et les voyages d'Orient.

Ces armes sont celles de la 1ère maison de Courtenay, dont plusieurs membres ont été sacrés empereurs latins d’Orient.
La seigneurie de Courtenay a eu pour fondateur au début du 11ème siècle Athon de Château-Renard, sir de Courtenay, descendant de Rainart, comte de Sens. Parmi ses membres, se sont illustrés principalement Jocelin 1er le Grand, seigneur de Tibériade en 1115, puis comte d’Edesse en 1119. Elisabeth qui épousa en 1150 Pierre 1er de France, 7ème fils du roi Louis VI, ce qui fit de la maison de Courtenay une branche de la dynastie royale de France (voir l’arbre généalogique en Mairie). Pierre II qui devint comte de Nevers, d’Auxerre, et de Tonnerre par son 1er mariage et empereur latin d’Orient en 1216 par son 2ème mariage. Robert 1er et Baudouin II qui furent aussi empereurs de Constantinople jusqu’en 1261, puis Catherine 1ère qui resta impératrice titulaire. Elle épousa Charles de Valois, le comté de Courtenay appartint ensuite aux Valois et passa dans le domaine royal.
Source :
Histoire de la maison de Courtenay
relevé JPR Teyssier Chalancon

Athon was succeeded by his son Joscelin, who had three sons: Miles, who was Lord of Courtenay after him; Joscelin, who joined the First Crusade and became Count of Edessa; and Geoffrey, who also fought in the Holy land and died there.

In the 12th century, Reginald de Courtenay (d.1190), son of Milo de Courtenay (d.1127), quarrelled with King Louis VII of France and moved to England: His French lands were forfeit, and passed, with his sister Elizabeth, to Louis’ brother Peter, who took the name "Peter de Courtenay".

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/House_of_Courtenay

Gâtinais (Loiret), Bourgogne, Ile-de-France, Angleterre

http://racineshistoire.free.fr/LGN/PDF/Courtenay.pdf

zh.lifeSummary: 當Milo de Courtenay於1068年出生在法國崔-瓦爾 德 洛易洛易特庫尼時，他的父親Joscelin de Courtenay seigneur de Courtenay34歲，母親Elisabeth Montlhéry28歲。他於大約1095年與Ermengard De Nevers在法國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1127年在他的家鄉享年59歲，埋葬在法國崔-瓦爾 德 洛易洛易特豐泰聶讓安。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Courtenay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Courtney  .

variants.name: Courtney
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Courtenay near Sens in northern France, or some other place similarly named, from the name of a Gallo-Roman landlord, Curtenus (a derivative of Latin curtus ‘short’) + the locative suffix -acum.
Irish: English surname adopted for Ó Curnáin ‘descendant of Curnán’, an Old Irish personal name based on a diminutive of corn ‘horn’.

History: A family of this name (see 1 above) can be traced back to Athon de Courtenay, who lived in Courtenay in Loiret in the early 11th century. Renaud de Courtenay held land in Devon in the 12th century, and the family later held the title of Earls of Devon following the marriage of Hugh de Courtenay to Margaret de Bohun, granddaughter of King Edward I, in 1325.

nameMeanings.name: Milo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of   Miles  , regularly used in documents of the Middle Ages, and revived as a given name in the 19th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Milo de Courtenay was born in 1068, in Courtenay, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, France, his father, Joscelin de Courtenay seigneur de Courtenay, was 34 and his mother, Elisabeth Montlhéry, was 28. He married Ermengard De Nevers about 1095, in France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in 1127, in his hometown, at the age of 59, and was buried in Fontainejean, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Milo de Courtenay est né en 1068, à Courtenay, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, France, son père, Joscelin de Courtenay seigneur de Courtenay, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Elisabeth Montlhéry, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Ermengard De Nevers environ 1095, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé en 1127, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à Fontainejean, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, France.

Er hat um 1095 in Frankreich Ermengard De Nevers geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. ko.lifeSummary: Milo de Courtenay(이)가 1068년 프랑스 세트레-발 데 로이르 로이렛 쿠르텐아이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Joscelin de Courtenay seigneur de Courtenay(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Elisabeth Montlhéry(은)는 28세였다. 그는 대략 1095년 프랑스에서 Ermengard De Nevers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1127년 그의 고향에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 세트레-발 데 로이르 로이렛 폰테인제이이에 묻혔다.

ru.Courtenay, Loiret, Centre, France: коуртенеи, лоирет, кентре-валь де лоир, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Milo de Courtenay родился в 1068 г. в местности коуртенеи, лоирет, кентре-валь де лоир, Франция, его отцу по имени Joscelin de Courtenay seigneur de Courtenay было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth Montlhéry, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Ermengard De Nevers около 1095 г. в местности Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер в 1127 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности фонтеинеджин, лоирет, кентре-валь де лоир, Франция.
